topicData.topic: A morning at the Subarnarekha where the water level dropped unexpectedly, revealing what had been hidden underneath.
topicData.angle: What does a river remember when it forgets to flood? The specifics of mud, plastic, and what locals said about last year versus this year.

postText:
The Subarnarekha dropped overnight.

Not a little. Enough that by morning there was a full shelf of exposed riverbed where yesterday there was only water.

First thing you notice is the mud. Dark, almost black, with this smell that hits before you see anything. Then the plastic. Nested into the silt like it had always been there. Bottles, wrappers, a sandal.

The locals said last year the water never went this low this early. One of the older men pointed to a line on the embankment stones. The river was here in June last year, he said. Look at it now.

I didn't have a good answer for that.

We pulled 500+ kg of waste during the cleanup. But standing on that exposed bed, looking at what was still there, that number felt small.

What the river holds when it floods is invisible. What it shows when it pulls back is harder to look away from.

I keep thinking about that line on the stone.

What are you seeing in rivers near you this season?
